raw_consumer_statement:
Product : Credit card ( XXXX XXXX XXXX issued by Citibank , N.A . ) Issue type : Billing error dispute / foreign transaction Summary of complaint : I am submitting a complaint regarding Citibank, N.A.s handling of a disputed foreign transaction on my XXXX XXXX XXXX credit card. 
The disputed charge involves an apparent currency denomination error and inconsistent merchant identification arising from a transaction in XXXX. At the time of purchase, I requested that the charge be processed in XXXX pesos, but it posted in U.S. dollars. I was not provided a receipt despite requesting one. 
I submitted a written dispute within the required time period and provided all information required under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z, including account identification, amount, and reasons for believing a billing error occurred. A Citi representative later acknowledged that my dispute was timely. However, Citi denied the dispute solely because I could not provide a receipt, even though the merchant did not provide one and Citi did not request transaction documentation from the merchant. 
The dispute falls within the statutory definition of a billing error, including an incorrect amount, improper identification, and a request for clarification and documentary evidence. The process applied appears to impose an evidentiary burden on the consumer that is impractical in foreign transactions where merchants fail to provide receipts. 
I request CFPB review of whether Citibanks investigation and resolution of this dispute complied with the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ), and whether its practices provide consumers a meaningful opportunity to contest incorrect charges.

Desired resolution : Review of Citibanks handling of this dispute for compliance with FCBA and Regulation Z, and appropriate corrective action.
